commit.message:
fix: add network/tools config to docs continuous improvement agentic workflow (#207)

* Initial plan

* fix: add network and tools config to docs_continuous_improvement workflow

The docs_continuous_improvement_every_3_days.md was missing the
'network: defaults' and 'tools: { edit:, bash: true }' frontmatter
sections that are required for the Copilot agent to have file-editing
permissions and network access.

Without 'tools: edit:', the <allowed-paths> section in the generated
prompt was empty, preventing the agent from editing any documentation
files. Without 'network: defaults', network access would be undefined.

Also update frontmatter_hash in the .lock.yml to reflect the updated
frontmatter. Note: the exact hash algorithm used by gh-aw compile could
not be determined without the gh-aw binary; the hash has been updated
to SHA-256 of the raw frontmatter text. Recompile with 'gh aw compile'
to regenerate the canonical hash if needed.

The compiled lock file body is unchanged since both network: defaults
and tools: { edit:, bash: true } were already reflected in the
compiled output via --allow-all-paths and ['defaults'] allowed domains.

Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: move report coaches to top of command routing

Moved report coaches check to be the very first command match
(before initiate pairings and judge lookup) so it can't be
intercepted by other handlers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v7 suite — final squeeze with pool_order=2 and fit_subsample

Build conv_cifar_v7 suite (9 configs) testing the two genuinely
untested dimensions remaining after 6 prior sweeps:

1. pool_order=2 (second-order pooling statistics): appends per-channel
   variance in each SPP spatial block, doubling the feature dimension.
   The quick sanity test shows 64.79% vs 52.18% (v6 quick) at 2k
   neurons — a dramatic improvement that suggests variance captures
   texture discrimination missed by mean pooling alone.  Test at
   20k (×1/×5/×10) and 30k ×5 with α=2e-2.

2. fit_subsample scaling: we've always used 10k images for PCA filter
   learning and center selection.  Test 20k to see if more diverse
   patches improve filter and center quality.  Test at 20k ×5 and
   30k ×5/×10.

Also includes 48×3 30k ×10 (scaling v5's best filter config with
v6's ×10 ensemble size) and a combined ord2 + sub=20k config.

v6 findings driving these choices:
- 78.25% new record (30k ×10 α=2e-2), +0.37% over v5
- K-means filters dramatically worse (-14%), PCA conclusively best
- Per-patch normalization broken for single models
- α=1.5e-2 < α=2e-2 at 30k — confirms 2e-2 optimal
- Plateau around 78% with all prior levers exhausted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: GIF range selection on timeline track

Previously the range selector was a separate 24px bar below the timeline,
making it impossible to discover and interact with. The user could only set
the playhead position on the main timeline, not a GIF range.

Now:
- Range selection is integrated directly into Timeline.vue
- In GIF mode: clicking the track sets start + auto-end (start+5s)
- Two full-height teal handles are rendered ON the timeline itself
- Handles use pointer capture so dragging stays responsive even when
  the cursor moves fast outside the element
- Dark overlay masks areas outside the selection; turns amber at 10s limit
- Duration badge shows inside the selection window
- Hint text 'Click to start selecting a range' shown when nothing selected
- The separate GifRangeSelector bar below the timeline is removed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[WIP] Fix entity identity issues with ObjectId and upsert behavior (#53)

* Initial plan

* Changes before error encountered

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ythril-network/Ythril/sessions/ae9f6b9a-d34a-4050-b5a4-0cbc85aa2acc

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: entity identity model — UUID-only IDs, ID-based upsert, no ghost stubs from remember

Defect 1: All write paths now generate UUID v4 (never ObjectId). Supplied IDs
are validated against UUID v4 regex and rejected with 400 if invalid.

Defect 2: upsertEntity uses _id as identity. With id → update by _id (or
insert with that _id). Without id → always insert new record. Name is a
non-unique searchable label.

Defect 3: remember no longer auto-creates ghost type:entity stubs. Entity names
are resolved via findEntitiesByName (no type constraint). Unresolved names
produce warnings; multiple matches link to all and warn.

Also adjusts bulk_write endpoints from main merge to use new identity model.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ythril-network/Ythril/sessions/e8eb3515-1335-459b-992d-61fbe30b3cd4

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(capture): support MP4 and WebM output formats (#73)

* feat(capture): support MP4 and WebM output formats (#38)

- Add gecho.recording.outputFormat config (gif|mp4|webm, default: gif)
- gif: existing palettegen+paletteuse pipeline (unchanged, backwards compat)
- mp4: rename temp capture in-place — no re-encode needed (already H.264)
- webm: re-encode with libvpx-vp9 via ffmpeg (-crf 33, constant quality)
- Save dialog adapts extension and file filter to chosen format
- Progress notification title adapts to format
- New src/converter/outputConverter.ts encapsulates format dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add CONTRIBUTING.md with testing policy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: merge decision inbox and finalize vex countdown

- Merged single inbox file (epoch-native-helpers) into decisions.md
- Deleted epoch-native-helpers.md from decisions/inbox/
- Updated vex/history.md: finalized PR #72 countdown work (#51), compiled clean, pushed to feat/51-countdown-before-gif
- Recorded team directive: all features/bugs/changes require tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(converter): full coverage for outputConverter — mp4, gif, webm paths

- mp4: rename happy path + rename failure
- gif: delegates to GifConverter, propagates rejection
- webm: sanitization rejection, ENOENT spawn error, exit-0 success, exit-1 failure with stderr

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(converter): exclude outputConverter tests from VS Code runner

outputConverter.test.ts uses vscodeMock to control getConfig() output.
In the VS Code extension host real vscode is available, so the mock is
skipped — getConfig() returns the real ffmpeg default path, causing
Ubuntu CI (which has ffmpeg installed) to run real ffmpeg against fake
fixture files.

Exclude from the extension host runner; tests run correctly via plain
Mocha where vscodeMock is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: merge origin/main into feat/38-output-formats

Co-authored-by: PalmEmanuel <3384251+PalmEmanuel@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: YouTube token JSON parsing + printf instead of echo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch emojis to 🤩😁😐😴🤮, add scoring, and build results reveal dashboard (#13)

Replace rating emojis with 🤩😁😐😴🤮 (scores: 5, 3, 1, 0, -1). When voting
closes, the dashboard becomes a full-screen reveal experience showing
results from worst to best, one score group at a time.

### Emoji & scoring (`constants.ts`)
- `RATINGS` updated to new emoji set with labels
- Added `RATING_SCORES` map for score computation

### Results computation (`data.ts`)
- `getResultsByScore()` aggregates all votes per song, computes total
score, groups songs by score, returns groups sorted ascending (worst
first)
- Each `SongResult` includes per-voter breakdown (emoji + score)

### Results reveal UI (`results-reveal.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`)
- Dashboard conditionally renders `ResultsReveal` when game is closed,
DJ controls when open
- Full-screen sections: splash → click to reveal each score group →
winner with 🏆 treatment
- Each section shows songs, total scores, and voter-by-voter emoji
breakdown

### E2E test (`voting.spec.ts`)
- Added "results reveal walks through score groups after voting is
closed" test covering the full reveal flow: casting votes with distinct
emojis on two songs, closing the game, navigating to the dashboard,
clicking through all score groups from worst to winner, and verifying
the final "All results revealed" state

```tsx
// Score mapping
export const RATING_SCORES: Record<RatingEmoji, number> = {
  "🤩": 5, "😁": 3, "😐": 1, "😴": 0, "🤮": -1,
};
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: include ROOT_PATH prefix in all login/logout redirects (#39)

RedirectResponse and form action were using bare paths (/login, /, /logout)
which broke navigation on API Gateway where the stage prefix /prod is required.

Changes:
- app/main.py: prepend root_path to all RedirectResponse URLs
- templates/login.html: form action uses {{ root_path }}/login
- templates/index.html: logout link uses {{ root_path }}/logout

fix: add basePath /mbrista for GitHub Pages asset paths

which 404s on GitHub Pages subdirectory deployments.
This caused the entire site to render as unstyled HTML on the live site.

commit.message:
feat: animated GIF mode

Backend (Rust/Actix):
- ffmpeg.rs: add generate_gif() — single-pass embedded-palette FFmpeg
  filter for best quality (lanczos scale + split palettegen/paletteuse)
- handlers/gif.rs: POST /api/gif/{session_id}
  - body: { start, end, quality: small|medium|high }
  - validates end-start ≤ 10s, start ≥ 0
  - quality presets: small(320px,10fps) medium(640px,15fps) high(960px,20fps)
  - streams GIF as attachment download, deletes temp file after
- main.rs + mod.rs: register new route
- 6 new unit tests for quality mapping and duration validation

Frontend (Nuxt/Vue):
- useGif.ts composable: range state, quality state, generateGif(), max 10s clamp
- GifRangeSelector.vue: two draggable pointer handles on timeline,
  enforces 10s max, shows duration label, amber border at limit
- GifControls.vue: quality preset toggle, generate button, spinner,
  GIF preview + download link, error display
- Timeline.vue: gifMode/gifStart/gifEnd props → teal selection overlay
  with dark mask outside the selection
- editor.vue: Photo / GIF mode toggle in header; GIF mode shows
  GifRangeSelector below timeline and GifControls in sidebar
- 10 new Vitest tests for useGif (range clamping, quality, API call)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Bulk write API for entities, edges, memories, and chrono entries (#48)

* Initial plan

* feat: add bulk write API for entities, edges, memories, and chrono entries

- Add POST /api/brain/spaces/:spaceId/bulk REST endpoint
  - Processes in order: memories → entities → edges → chrono
  - Per-item validation errors reported in errors[] without aborting batch
  - Returns { inserted, updated, errors } with counts per type
  - Capped at 500 entries per type
  - Proxy space support via ?targetSpace query param

- Add bulk_write MCP tool in mcp/router.ts
  - Same four arrays as REST API
  - Added to MUTATING_TOOLS set (blocked for read-only tokens)
  - Returns summary with inserted/updated/error counts

- Add integration tests in testing/integration/brain.test.js
  - 207 response with correct shape
  - Insert vs update tracking for entities/edges
  - Cross-type batch (entities created before edges)
  - Per-item validation errors don't abort batch
  - Empty body/arrays no-op
  - 404 for unknown space, 401 without auth

- Update docs/integration-guide.md with new endpoint and MCP tool

commit.message:
Clarify PayPal API URL - not from dashboard, just set in Render

Updated guide to make clear:
- PAYPAL_API_URL is NOT in PayPal dashboard
- It's a hardcoded URL for the backend
- Just copy-paste: https://api.sandbox.paypal.com (testing)
- Change to: https://api.paypal.com (production)

Only 2 things come FROM PayPal:
1. Client ID
2. Client Secret

API URL is just set in Render env vars.

commit.message:
feat(commands): add open, put, take-from container commands (#409)

Implement Phase 4 container commands:
- open <container>: shows contents, slot usage, weight capacity
- put <item> in <container>: moves item from inventory into container
- take <item> from <container>: removes item from container to inventory

All commands use the shared pure container functions (addItemToContainer,
removeItemFromContainer). Validates slot limits, weight limits, allowed
item types, and nesting prevention.

29 new tests covering all success paths and error cases.
2915 total server tests passing, zero regressions.

commit.message:
fix: Admit Patient modal UX — required indicators, inline helper, error banner, loading state

Root cause: button disabled={!admitAnimal} with zero UI explanation — user had no way to know
they needed to type AND select from the dropdown, not just type.

Changes:
- Added admitError + admitSubmitting state
- handleAdmit: catch block now surfaces API errors to user (was silent empty catch)
- Modal: ✕ close button in header + overlay-click-to-close
- AnimalSearchPicker: amber warning box below field when no animal selected
  '⚠️ Required: Type an animal name and select a patient from the dropdown'
- All required fields: red * marker
- All optional fields: (optional) label
- Required field legend at bottom of form
- Submit button: disabled title tooltip + '⏳ Admitting...' loading state
- API error shown as red banner at top of modal

Also:
- Expanded Usability Standards Rule in copilot-instructions.md to be mandatory/exhaustive
- Logged LESSON-048 to memories/repo/lessons.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Top 5 performance optimizations from audit

1. Pathfinding: cache off-road edges at world gen (O(n²) → O(1) per call),
   binary heap Dijkstra (O(V²) → O((V+E)logV)), path result caching

2. Expand _tickCache: add eliteMerchants[], merchantsByKingdom[]. Replace
   20+ world.people.filter() calls in kingdom/tax/military tick with cache
   lookups (soldiers, citizens, EMs, merchants)

3. Replace all 122 JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) with structuredClone()
   in serialization and throughout player.js (2-3x faster deep clones)

4. Convert O(n) includes()/indexOf()/find() to O(1) Set.has() in daily
   tick: satQuitters, wage quitters, employee validation, guild seizure,
   grace period cleanup. Also fix financialLedger shift() → splice()

5. UI: reduce auto-refresh from 1s to 3s with dirty-flag day-change
   detection for immediate refresh when needed. Export markPanelDirty()

Bonus: incremental _militarySalesTotal counter replaces daily tradeLog scan

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

implementation blog post and replace it with a link to the dedicated
Multi-Head Attention theory blog in the attention section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add --warnings / -W flag to control warning display

Warnings (both Oscan semantic and C compiler) are now suppressed by
default. Pass --warnings or -W to see them. This applies to:
- Resource leak warnings from the semantic analyzer
- C compiler warnings (via -w / /w flag, conditional on --warnings)
- C compiler stderr on successful compilation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: constrain overlay position and size on viewport resize

- Add window resize listener in overlayPlayer.open(), removed in close()
- constrainOverlay: scale down only when overlay actually exceeds viewport,
  then clamp position to keep overlay fully in viewport (150ms debounce)
- Maximized state: recalculate maximized size against new viewport on resize
- restore button: call constrainOverlay after restoring savedState
- reset button: call constrainOverlay after applying initW/initH
- Prevents the jarring jump when dragging an out-of-viewport overlay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: 精简重构为专注翻译的插件

- 翻译服务精简为: Google/Microsoft免费 + 阿里百炼/DeepL/Gemini/OpenRouter/DeepSeek/GLM/Kimi/OpenAI/Claude + 自定义
- 新增6种翻译Prompt预设(通用/新闻/学术/技术/文学/社交)
- DeepL支持免费/付费API切换
- 全新现代UI设计: popup极简单列布局, options手风琴折叠式服务配置
- 修复content.js语法错误导致悬浮按钮消失
- 扩展翻译选择器覆盖blockquote/figcaption/td/div等元素

commit.message:
Add complete payment setup master guide

Master guide coordinating both payment methods:
- Razorpay (UPI for India) - 30 min setup
- PayPal (International) - 20 min setup

Timeline from deployment to live dual-payments: 90 minutes
Recommended order: Deploy → Razorpay → PayPal → Go Live

Includes:
- Both setup paths (Razorpay-first or PayPal-first)
- Minute-by-minute timeline
- Customer checkout flow
- Environment variables needed
- Security & testing checklists
- Helpful links and documents reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
